item.title: Bangladesh lose to New Zealand in 3rd T20I
item.description: New Zealand levelled the three-match T20I series 1-1 when they beat Bangladesh by six wickets in DLS method in the rain-curtailed third and final game at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium in Mirpur on Saturday. ...

item.title: BNP-backed lawyers sweep Dhaka Bar polls amid AL ban
item.description: The Bangladesh Jatiyatabadi Ainjibi Oikya Panel, backed by the ruling BNP, swept the Dhaka Bar Association election for the 2026–2027 term, winning all 23 executive posts, including president and general secretary....

item.title: BNP-backed lawyers sweep Dhaka Bar polls amid AL ban
item.description: The Bangladesh Jatiyatabadi Ainjibi Oikya Panel, backed by the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party, swept the Dhaka Bar Association election for the 2026–2027 term, winning all 23 executive posts, including the posts of president and general secretary......Global headlines service

item.title: Prime minister Tarique Rahman inaugurates Notun Kuri Sports in Sylhet
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman inaugurated the Notun Kuri Sports programme in Sylhet on Saturday to help create new athletes, and urged all — irrespective ofpoliticalaffiliation — to work together to strengthen the country’s sports sector......

item.title: Former lawmaker, AL leader Moslem Uddin dies
item.description: Former five-time lawmaker from Mymensingh-6 (Fulbaria) constituency and Awami League leader Advocate Moslem Uddin passed away on Saturday morning......

item.title: Stricter anti-corruption measures in DPL: Tamim
item.description:
The Bangladesh Cricket Board would introduce strict anti-corruption protocols in the forthcoming Dhaka Premier Division Cricket League, stated the president of the ad-hoc committee, Tamim Iqbal, on Saturday.
...

item.title: Prime minister Tarique Rahman seeks public support to take Bangladesh forward
item.description: Saying that his government’s main responsibility is to protect the interests of people and resolve their problems, prime minister Tarique Rahman on Saturday urged citizens of all strata to stand by his government to help them taking Bangladesh forward and placing it alongside developed nations......Diaspora community forum

item.title: Mourinho says no contact with Real Madrid
item.description:
Benfica coach Jose Mourinho said Friday there has been no contact between him and Real Madrid, despite speculation the Spanish giants could turn to him this summer.
...

item.title: Bangladesh all out for 102 in third T20I
Russian employee of Rooppur Nuclear Power Plant project dies in Pabna
item.description: A Russian national died on Friday after falling sick while exercising in the residential area of Rooppur Nuclear Power Plant in Ishwardi upazila of Pabna......

item.title: Nomination of NCP’s Nusrat declared valid for reserved JS seat
item.description: The returning officer has declared the nomination paper of National Citizen Party joint-convener Nusrat Tabassum valid for a reserved seat for women in the parliament......Breakingnews alert

item.title: PM declares plan to improve road, rail communications between Dhaka, Sylhet
item.description: Recalling his difficult road journey back to Dhaka from Sylhet during the election campaign, prime minister Tarique Rahman on Saturday said that his government had started working to improve both road and rail connectivity between the two districts to make travel faster and more comfortable......Trendingnews digests

item.title: CAC’s May Day event calls for workers’ rights
item.description: A discussion marking International Workers’ Day was held in Dhaka on Friday, with speakers calling for stronger workers’ rights, safer workplaces and a corruption-free Bangladesh......

item.title: Senior Iranian military officer says renewed war with United States ‘likely’
item.description: A senior Iranian military officer said on Saturday that renewed fighting between the United States and Iran was ‘likely’, hours after president Donald Trump said he was ‘not satisfied’ with a new Iranian negotiating proposal......
